sections.title: DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
sections.text: This Standard applies to commercial sweet varieties of cassava roots grown from Manihot esculenta Crantz, of the Euphorbiaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Cassava for industrial processing is excluded.
sections.retrieval_text:
DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
number=1
level=1
This Standard applies to commercial sweet varieties of cassava roots grown from Manihot esculenta Crantz, of the Euphorbiaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Cassava for industrial processing is excluded.

page_spans.text: This Standard applies to commercial sweet varieties of cassava roots grown from Manihot esculenta Crantz, of the Euphorbiaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Cassava for industrial processing is excluded.

sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY
number=2

sections.title: MINIMUM REQUIREMENTS
CLASSIFICATION
sections.text: Cassava is classified in three classes defined below:
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY > CLASSIFICATION
number=2.2
level=2
Cassava is classified in three classes defined below:

page_spans.text: Cassava is classified in three classes defined below:

sections.text:
The cassava must have reached an appropriate degree of physiological development account being taken of the characteristics of the variety and the area in which they are grown. The development and condition of the cassava must be such as to enable it:
­ to withstand transport and handling; and
­ to arrive in satisfactory condition at the place of destination.

level=3

page_spans.text:
The cassava must have reached an appropriate degree of physiological development account being taken of the characteristics of the variety and the area in which they are grown. The development and condition of the cassava must be such as to enable it:
­ to withstand transport and handling; and
­ to arrive in satisfactory condition at the place of destination.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING SIZING
sections.text:
Size is determined by the diameter at thickest cross-section of the produce, in accordance with the following table:
Size Code Diameter
(in centimetres)
A 3.5 – 6.0
B 6.1 – 8.0
C > 8.0
In all cases, cassava must not be less than 300 g in weight nor less than 20 cm in length.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING TOLERANCES
sections.text: Tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each package for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated.
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING TOLERANCES
number=4
level=1
Tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each package for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated.

page_spans.text: Tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each package for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated.

sections.title: “Extra” Class
sections.text: Cassava in this class must be of superior quality. It must be characteristic of the variety and/or commercial type. It must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.
sections.retrieval_text:
CLASSIFICATION > “Extra” Class
number=2.2.1
level=2
Cassava in this class must be of superior quality. It must be characteristic of the variety and/or commercial type. It must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

page_spans.text: Cassava in this class must be of superior quality. It must be characteristic of the variety and/or commercial type. It must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

sections.title: Class I
sections.retrieval_text:
CLASSIFICATION > Class I
number=2.2.2
level=2
Cassava in this class must be of good quality. It must be characteristic of the variety and/or commercial type. The following slight defects, however, may be allowed, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package:
- slight defects in shape;
- scarring or healed damage, not exceeding 5% of the surface area;
- scraped areas, not exceeding 10% of the surface area.
The defects must not, in any case, affect the pulp of the produce.

sections.title: Class II
sections.retrieval_text:
CLASSIFICATION > Class II
number=2.2.3
level=2
This class includes cassava which does not qualify for inclusion in the higher classes, but satisfy the minimum requirements specified in Section 2.1 above. The following defects, however, may be allowed, provided the cassava retains its essential characteristics as regards the quality, the keeping quality and presentation:
- defects in shape;
- scarring or healed damage, not exceeding 10% of the surface area;
- scraped areas, not exceeding 20% of the surface area.
The defects must not, in any case, affect the pulp of the produce.

sections.title: UNIFORMITY
sections.text: The contents of each package must be uniform in shape and contain only cassava of the same origin, variety and/or commercial type, quality and size. The visible part of the contents of the package must be representative of the entire contents.

sections.title: PACKAGING
sections.text: Cassava must be packed in such a way as to protect the produce properly. The materials used inside the package must be new4, clean, and of a quality such as to avoid causing any external or internal damage to the produce. The use of materials, particularly of paper or stamps bearing trade specifications is allowed, provided the printing or labelling has been done with non-toxic ink or glue. Cassava shall be packed in each container in compliance with the Code of Practice for Packaging and Transport of Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CAC/RCP 44-1995).

pages.title: STANDARD FOR SWEET CASSAVA (CODEX STAN 238-2003)
pages.summary: Página 1 contém o título do documento e cabeçalho CODEX STAN 238, com duas seções principais: '1. DEFINITION OF PRODUCE' com texto explicativo e '2. PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY' que abriga subseções '2.1 MINIMUM REQUIREMENTS' (com conteúdo detalhado e uma subsubseção 2.1.1 sem título visual) e '2.2 CLASSIFICATION', marcando continuidade para página seguinte.

pages.title: CODEX STAN 238
pages.summary: A página 2 continua a seção 2.2 CLASSIFICATION iniciada na página anterior, detalhando as classes 2.2.1, 2.2.2 e 2.2.3 com texto explicativo e critérios. Em seguida, iniciam-se novas seções de nível 1 3 PROVISIONS CONCERNING SIZING e 4 PROVISIONS CONCERNING TOLERANCES com subníveis 4.1 QUALITY TOLERANCES e 4.1.1 “Extra” Class. Marcadores de continuidade foram definidos para refletir segmento iniciado ou em andamento na página ou continuam na próxima.

pages.title: CODEX STAN 238
pages.summary: A página 3 continua a seção 4 PROVISIONS CONCERNING TOLERANCES iniciada na página anterior, detalhando as classes 4.1.2 e 4.1.3 com textos explicativos. Em seguida, apresenta seção 4.2 SIZE TOLERANCES e inicia a seção 5 PROVISIONS CONCERNING PRESENTATION com seus subtítulos e detalhamentos. Finaliza com a seção 6 MARKING OR LABELLING e seus subníveis. Todos os títulos numéricos e em caixa alta foram mapeados corretamente, texto associado claramente aos respectivos nós, sem inclusão de rodapés ou cabeçalhos repetitivos. Não há evidências de que alguma seção continue na próxima página nem que conteúdos venham da anterior além do indicado.

pages.title: CODEX STAN 238
pages.summary: A página 4 contém o término dos subtítulos da seção 6 MARKING OR LABELLING iniciada em página anterior, evidenciado por is_continuation_from_previous_page=true nesses nós. Em seguida expõe as seções principais 7 CONTAMINANTS e 8 HYGIENE com seus respectivos subtítulos. O cabeçalho na página é identificado como CODEX STAN 238 Page 4 of 4 e o título do documento como CODEX STAN 238. Os textos foram alocados conforme hierarquia numérica e estrutural correta, sem inclusão de rodapé ou cabeçalho repetitivo. Não há indicações de continuidade para próxima página e a estrutura está consistente para uso em RAG.
